items.title:
The most important shift in AI isn’t model quality. 
 
It’s compute scarcity. 
 
$GOOGL x SpaceX deal highlights a simple truth: 
 
Even hyperscalers are competing for external capacity. 
 
If that continues, the biggest winners will be: 
 
memory ($MU, $SNDK) 
 
compute ($NVDA, $TSM) 
 
The questio

items.title: Nvidia Just Slipped Below $5 Trillion. These Are the Few Companies With a Realistic Shot at Catching It.
items.body: Nvidia has slipped below $5 trillion in market value but remains the world's most valuable company. Three competitors—Alphabet, Apple, and Microsoft—are positioned as potential challengers, though each would need specific favorable conditions and years of strong growth to overtake the AI chip leader
items.author: The Motley Fool

items.title: Don't Buy Home Depot Stock Until You Know This 1 Key Metric
items.body: Home Depot's stock has fallen 28% from its peak, but investors should be cautious. The key metric to watch is same-store sales, which grew only 0.6% in Q1 fiscal 2026, following weak growth of 0.3% in the prior year and a 1.8% decline the year before. items.title: What Happens to Your 401(k) and IRA When You Die -- What Heirs Need to Know
items.body: The article explains how inherited 401(k)s and IRAs are treated differently depending on the heir's relationship to the deceased. Surviving spouses have more flexibility with rollover options, while non-spouse beneficiaries must transfer assets to inherited IRAs and typically withdraw funds within 1

items.title: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Dell, GameStop, Lululemon — And Stocks Take A Hit On Interest Rate Hike Fears
items.body:
Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

items.title: Why LinkedIn Founder Reid Hoffman Is Leaving Microsoft's Board After Nearly A Decade
items.body:
Reid Hoffman, who co-founded LinkedIn in 2002, is stepping down from Microsoft‘s (NASDAQ:MSFT) board of directors to refocus on Manus, his AI-powered drug discovery startup, the company announced Thursday.



LinkedIn Founder Returns to Startup Roots

items.title: Warren Buffett disciple Guy Spier built career on value investing. A rare cancer changed everything
items.body: After a glioblastoma diagnosis, value investor Guy Spier rethought wealth, time and legacy — and the impact he can have on rare disease research.
